title: Indulgent Basque French Pork Cassoulet
description: A rich and flavorful French dish featuring pork, gochujang, and a crispy crust, all made in an iron teppan and perfect for a special occasion. This low-carb-friendly recipe is sure to impress.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the gochujang, duck fat, and tomato paste.
Add the pork pieces to the teppan and sear until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped onion to the teppan and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the teppan and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth, thyme, and bay leaves to the teppan and bring to a simmer.
Cover the teppan and transfer to the oven to cook for 2 hours.
Remove the teppan from the oven and let cool slightly.
Dehydrate the cassoulet in a low temperature oven until the top is crispy and the liquid has thickened into a glassy jelly.
